page: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, delivering 96 dedicated virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX-512 and Intel Deep Learning Boost (VNNI) instruction sets.

The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local instance storage, relying entirely on network-attached Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes. To support high-throughput storage and clustering workloads, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the m5n.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core performance, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU suites, and database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with cached and uncached read/write operations consistently placing in the upper quartile. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also show strong results. However, single-threaded performance is more moderate, with average scores in Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests, and weaker throughput in single-threaded gzip compression.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5n.24xlarge offers a high-density resource profile tailored for network-heavy and distributed applications without the overhead of local SSDs. It is highly suited for large-scale enterprise applications, database hosting, Redis caching layers, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that leverage its 100 Gbps network interface.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 3.1 GHz, supported by 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. While it lacks local storage, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, making it highly optimized for EBS and network-intensive operations. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent throughput for database and Redis operations, though single-threaded performance remains average. This instance is well-suited for high-throughput web serving, enterprise databases, caching layers, and distributed multi-threaded workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, general-purpose instance featuring ninety-six dedicated virtual cores and one hundred gigabits per second of baseline network throughput.

Baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps optimized for EBS and clustering
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Excellent throughput for Redis and database operations
No local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage
Suited for enterprise databases, caching layers, and web serving
